text: Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. One key principle is responsive design, which ensures the app works well on different screen sizes, from small phones to large tablets. Developers must decide between building a native app, which is specifically designed for one platform like iOS or Android, or a cross-platform app, which uses a single codebase for both. Native apps often offer better performance, but cross-platform tools save time and money. Once the app is ready, it must be submitted to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store for review and distribution.
